lede: A look at December 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Syd Hartha and François Hamelin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Argentina national football team
events.desc: Argentina win the FIFA World Cup final, defeating title holders France 4–2 on penalties following a 3–3 draw after extra time.

events.title: Amtrak Cascades
events.desc: Amtrak Cascades passenger train 501, derailed near DuPont, Washington, a city in United States near Olympia, Washington killing six people, and injuring 70 others.

events.title: Chadian Civil War (2005–2010)
events.desc: The Chadian Civil War begins when rebel groups, allegedly backed by neighbouring Sudan, launch an attack in Adré.

events.title: United Airlines Flight 2860
events.desc: United Airlines Flight 2860 crashes near Kaysville, Utah, killing all three crew members on board.

events.title: SCORE (satellite)
events.desc: Project SCORE, the world's first communications satellite, is launched.

events.title: Tornado outbreak sequence of December 18–20, 1957
events.desc: A violent F5 tornado wipes out the entire community of Sunfield, Illinois.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of the Heligoland Bight, the first major air battle of the war, takes place.

events.title: Eighteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
events.desc: The resolution containing the language of the Eighteenth Amendment to enact Prohibition is passed by the United States Congress.

events.title: 1867 Keelung earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 7.0 earthquakes strikes off the coast of Taiwan, triggering a tsunami and killing at least 580 people.

events.title: Thanksgiving (United States)
events.desc: The United States celebrates its first Thanksgiving, marking the recent victory by the American rebels over British General John Burgoyne at Saratoga in October.

births.name: Jules Dassin
births.line: American-Greek actor, director, producer, and screenwriter (died 2008)

deaths.text: Jim Hunt, American politician, 69th and 71st Governor of North Carolina
Alan Voorhees, American engineer and urban planner
José Justo Corro, Mexican politician, President of Mexico (1836–1837)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Expectation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
